scenario: Lumen Health, a clinic chain running 6800 triage evaluations per day in Camunda 8.8, deploys multiple DMN files. The platform team needs each Decision Requirements Graph to have a unique technical identifier set exactly where DMN defines it. The platform serves 12 tenants and processes 8400 transactions per day.
question: Where is the unique technical identifier of a DMN Decision Requirements Graph set in the XML?

options.text: In the id attribute on the definitions element, which serves as the unique technical identifier of the DRG at deployment
In the id attribute on the first decision element, which serves as the unique technical identifier of the DRG at deployment
In the id attribute on a drg element nested inside definitions, which serves as the unique technical identifier at deployment
In the namespace attribute on the definitions element, which serves as the unique technical identifier of the DRG at deployment

a.text: Correct. The DRG ID is the technical identifier of the DRG and is set in the id attribute on the definitions element; each DRG should have a unique ID at deployment.

b.text: Incorrect. The id on a decision element identifies that decision, not the enclosing DRG; the DRG ID lives on the definitions element.

c.text: Incorrect. DMN has no nested drg element; the DRG identifier is the id attribute on the root definitions element.

d.text: Incorrect. The namespace attribute declares an XML namespace, not the DRG's technical identifier; that identifier is the id attribute on definitions.

explanation: The DRG ID is the technical identifier of the DRG, set as the id attribute on the definitions element, and must be unique at deployment.
